top_signals.title: Iran says retaliatory attacks “legitimate and definite” if U.S. violates ceasefire
top_signals.summary: Iran’s Islamic Revoluationary Guard Corps has reportedly warned the U.S. that it considers retaliatory strikes “legitimate and definite” should the U.S. violate an ongoing ceasefire agreement, as tensions once again intensify in the Middle East, denting hopes for an imminent peace deal. Media reports citing Tasnim, a news agency closely linked to the IRGC, added ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: LNG shipping stocks: Stalemate continues
top_signals.summary: Summary The UP World LNG Shipping Index gained 0.11 points (0.05%) last week, closing at 214.82 points, while the S&P 500 gained 0.88% — its eighth consecutive weekly gain, supported by optimism around Middle East peace negotiations. top_signals.title: Shanghai sets target to build intl shipping hub
top_signals.summary: As Shanghai moves from scale expansion to capacity enhancement in building an international shipping center during the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-30) period, demand for talent with cross-disciplinary expertise is rising, according to experts. The port of Shanghai, the world’s largest container port for 16 consecutive years, handled 55.06 million twenty-foot equivalent units (TEUs) throughout 2025, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Without containers, the Netherlands would grind to a halt
top_signals.summary: Sixty years after the introduction of the container, many Dutch people are still unaware of how much we rely on container transport. This is revealed by new research commissioned by the Port of Rotterdam Authority and conducted among more than 1,500 respondents. Containers are still often seen as ‘boxes full of stuff’, whereas in reality ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bitget launches RWA platform Reality in tokenization push
top_signals.summary: Bitget said Reality will initially offer tokenized exposure to selected U.S. stocks and ETFs through its broader trading ecosystem.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: IMO MSC 111: New Mass Code Adopted
top_signals.summary: The 111th session of the Maritime Safety Committee (MSC 111) was held from 13 to 22 May 2026. The adoption of a new goal-based Code for Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships (MASS) marks a key milestone in the IMO’s work to ensure that the regulatory framework keeps pace with the rapid development of shipping technologies. MSC ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Countdown: CEO reflections on IMO progress and regulation’s role in maritime decarbonization
top_signals.summary: When we started the Center in 2020, the industry was asking whether decarbonizing shipping was possible. Today, the ecosystem is no longer asking that question. Companies have set ambitious targets and are implementing low-emissions technologies in their fleets. We have moved from asking whether shipping can decarbonize to asking how and when. In that time, ...

top_signals.title: Philip R. Lane: Interview with Nikkei
Guidance for Vessel Transit through the Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Hundreds of vessels remain unable to transit the Strait of Hormuz and, in the event of a return to more normal navigation conditions, the movement of all those vessels within the Strait could represent a considerable navigational hazard. To help mitigate the risk, the industry organisations ICS, BIMCO, INTERCARGO, INTERTANKO, IMCA, and OCIMF have worked ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Marine Lubricants: A Hidden Seaworthiness Risk
top_signals.summary: This Risk Alert highlights the importance of exercising due diligence in the procurement and onboard management of marine lubricants, particularly in the context of potential supply chain disruptions. Marine lubricants are vital for the functioning of a vessel’s propulsion, power generation, and auxiliary machinery. Inadequate lubrication can result in increased wear, reduced performance, or even ...
